vulnerability.reference: Underrepresentation of low-degree nodes in training data causes systematic under-recommendation, reinforcing social isolation.
vulnerability.generated: Low-degree users have sparse connections, reducing their algorithmic visibility in degree-biased link prediction.

impact.reference: Marginalized users experience diminished social capital and fewer opportunities for diverse connections.
impact.generated: Low-degree users receive fewer friend suggestions, entrenching social network inequality and limiting discovery of diverse connections.

dose_response.reference: Overfitting to node degree biases predictions toward high-degree nodes, which in social media friend suggestions exposes low-degree users to under-recommendation, leading to diminished social capital.
dose_response.generated: Link prediction models overfitting to node degree, through friend suggestions on social platforms, triggers reduced algorithmic visibility for low-degree users' sparse connections, leading to fewer suggestions that entrench inequality and limit diverse connection discovery.
